#### 3. Real-Time Updates

Any payment entry, partial payment, or adjustment updates the calendar instantly.

#### 4. Easy Month Navigation

Users can move between months to view:

- Upcoming supplier payments
- Overdue amounts
- Monthly payable projections

### Benefits

- Improves planning of supplier payments
- Enhances cashflow control
- Ensures timely settlement of supplier invoices
- Avoids missing due dates or late payment charges
- Strengthens vendor relations through timely payments

entries.title: DIGITZ ERP — Period Closing Voucher
entries.content:
The **Period Closing Voucher** in DIGITZ ERP helps organisations formally close their accounts for a defined accounting period — typically **quarterly, half-yearly, or yearly** as per company policy.

During closing, the system settles all Profit & Loss balances into the Balance Sheet, ensuring accurate financial reporting and preventing further changes to the closed period.

### Purpose of Period Closing

Period closing ensures the company's financial position for the selected period is finalised and protected from accidental or unauthorised modifications.

| What it Provides | Why it Matters |
| --- | --- |
| Transfers P&L balance into Balance Sheet | Finalises financial results for the period |
| Locks the accounting period | Prevents back-dated voucher edits or submissions |
| Strengthens financial accuracy | Supports audit and compliance requirements |
| Requires tax and reconciliation checks | Ensures correct tax reporting and filing |

### How Period Closing Works

#### 1. Select the Period

The user selects a **From Date** and **To Date**, defining the accounting period being closed.

#### 2. Review and Finalise All Transactions

Before closing the period, the organisation must manually ensure the following tasks are completed:

**Manual Tax Reconciliation (Required)**

- Reconcile VAT/GST collected & paid
- Verify tax ledgers (input tax, output tax, adjustments)
- Confirm that taxable transactions match the tax summary
- Ensure no missing or incorrect tax entries exist
- Reconcile with external systems or portals if required (e.g., FTA Portal – UAE)

*This manual tax reconciliation must be completed before the Period Closing Voucher is submitted.*

**General Reconciliation Checks**

- Bank reconciliation
- Vendor & customer reconciliation
- Ledger matching
- Final review of journals and adjustments

#### 3. System Settlement of Profit & Loss

Once submitted, DIGITZ ERP:

- Identifies all Profit & Loss accounts
- Calculates net profit or loss for the selected period
- Automatically transfers the value to the chosen Balance Sheet account (e.g., Retained Earnings)

#### 4. Locking the Period

After submission:

- No new transactions can be posted in the closed period
- No edits or cancellations are allowed
- Ensures historical accuracy and audit compliance

### Why Tax Reconciliation Must Be Manual

DIGITZ ERP intentionally **does not automate tax reconciliation** during period closing because:

- Tax computations may require cross-checking with government portals
- Some adjustments may be handled outside the ERP
- Tax filing rules vary between countries and reporting periods
- Manual review ensures accuracy and prevents incorrect tax reporting

This makes the process compliant, safe, and audit-ready.

### Typical Use Cases

- Quarterly closing for management performance review
- Half-year and annual closing for audit and financial reporting
- Preparing books before statutory tax filing
- Locking historical data to maintain consistency

### Future Enhancements *(Planned)*

- **Taxation Closing** *(Future Enhancement)* — Automated tax settlement and tax-period computations.
- **Voucher Edit Locking** *(Future Enhancement)* — Advanced locking rules for vouchers within closed periods.
- **Extended Compliance Tools** *(Future Enhancement)* — Automated reconciliation aids for tax and financial audits.

entries.title: DIGITZ Tracker — Overview
entries.content:
DIGITZ Tracker automates the tracking of every document that can expire — trade licenses, visas, insurance policies, contracts, certifications, vehicle registrations, and anything else your business depends on. Register a document once, and Tracker watches it forever: it raises alerts ahead of expiry, assigns an employee to take action, and tracks the renewal through clear statuses until it is closed.
          
            One-time registration, lifetime tracking. Unlike reminder apps, a Tracker document never "runs out" — every renewal automatically schedules the next expiry cycle, so nothing has to be re-entered.
          
          
            Automated Expiry AlertsEscalating notifications at 90, 60, 30, and 7 days before expiry — configurable per document type.
            Action OwnershipEvery expiring document is assigned to a responsible employee, with escalation if no action is taken.
            Status-Driven TrackingDocuments move through clear statuses, so you always know exactly where each renewal stands.
            Complete HistoryEvery renewal, alert, and action is logged against the document — an audit trail for compliance.

entries.title: DIGITZ Tracker — Key Concepts
entries.content:
Tracked Document
          The central record in DIGITZ Tracker. It holds the document's identity (type, number, issuing authority), its current expiry date, the responsible employee, and its status. A tracked document is registered once and lives forever, accumulating its renewal history.
          Document Type
          A reusable template that defines how a category of documents behaves — its alert schedule, default assignee rules, and renewal checklist. Examples: Trade License, Employee Visa, Vehicle Registration.
          Alert Schedule
          The set of reminder points before expiry. Each alert can notify the assignee, their manager, or a distribution list — by email, WhatsApp, or in-app notification.

entries.title: DIGITZ Tracker — Quick Start
entries.content:
From zero to your first tracked document in five steps:
          
            Create your Document TypesDefine the categories you need — e.g. Trade License, Visa, Insurance — each with its own alert schedule.
            Register your documentsEnter each document once: number, issuing authority, expiry date, and attach a scanned copy.
            Assign ownersPick the employee responsible for each document. They'll receive every alert and own the renewal.
            Let Tracker watchAlerts fire automatically as expiry approaches. No further data entry is needed.
            Close the renewalWhen renewed, the owner updates the new expiry date — Tracker archives the cycle and starts watching the next one.

entries.title: DIGITZ Tracker — Document Registration
entries.content:
Registration is a one-time activity per document. Each record captures the essentials — document type, reference number, issuing authority, issue and expiry dates, attachments — plus any custom fields your document type defines.
          
            Bulk import: load your existing document register from a spreadsheet in one go.
            Attachments: keep the scanned document with the record, so the latest copy is always one click away.
            Linked entities: connect a document to an employee, vehicle, company, or branch for filtered views.

entries.title: DIGITZ Tracker — Expiry Automation & Alerts
entries.content:
Tracker's scheduler evaluates every active document daily. As a document approaches expiry, alerts fire according to the document type's schedule, and the document's status moves automatically so dashboards always reflect reality.
          
            Escalation built in. If a document reaches a critical window with no action recorded, Tracker escalates to the assignee's manager automatically.

entries.title: DIGITZ Tracker — Employee Assignments
entries.content: Every document has exactly one accountable owner. Assignment can be manual, or rule-based per document type — for example, all visas to the HR officer, all vehicle registrations to the fleet supervisor. Owners see their pending actions in a personal worklist, and reassignment keeps the full history intact.

entries.title: DIGITZ Tracker — Status Tracking
entries.content:
Statuses are the heartbeat of Tracker. They change automatically with time and manually with user actions:
          
            
              StatusMeaningSet by
              ActiveDocument is valid and outside any alert window.Automatic
              Expiring SoonDocument has entered its alert window; owner has been notified.Automatic
              Renewal in ProgressOwner has started the renewal and logged an action.Owner
              ExpiredExpiry date has passed without a completed renewal.Automatic
              RenewedNew expiry recorded; cycle archived and tracking restarts.Owner

entries.title: DIGITZ Tracker — Renewal Workflow
entries.content:
When a renewal completes, the owner records the new expiry date and attaches the renewed document. Tracker then:
          
            Archives the completed cycle into the document's renewal history,
            Resets the status to Active, and
            Schedules the next alert cycle against the new expiry date — automatically.
          
          This is what makes registration truly one-time: the document keeps tracking itself for as long as your business holds it.
